title: Import and export master data
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to import and export master data such as Company, Department, Location, Group, and Employee records, so that I can migrate and bulk-maintain organizational reference data instead of entering it manually.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the Data Management screen, when I select a master data entity (Company, Department, Location, Group, or Employee), then I can choose to import or export records for that entity.; Given an export request for any master entity, when the export completes, then a downloadable file containing the current records for that entity is produced in the selected format.; Given an import file for a master entity, when I submit it, then records are created or updated for that entity subject to validation, sequencing, and rollback rules.; Given master data supports migration and bulk maintenance, when I use import/export, then all five master entities (Company, Department, Location, Group, Employee) are individually supported.; Given an import references a master that does not yet exist, when the import runs, then the dependency sequencing (Foundation → Organizational → Workforce → Transactional) determines whether the record can be processed.

title: Import and export transactional data
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to import and export transactional data such as Leaves and Attendance, so that I can load historical operational data in bulk and exchange it with external systems.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the Data Management screen, when I select a transactional data type (Leaves or Attendance), then I can import or export records for it.; Given an import file of Leave or Attendance records, when I submit it, then the transactional records are ingested subject to validation and rollback rules.; Given an export request for Leave or Attendance data, when it completes, then a downloadable file of those records is produced in the selected format.; Given transactional data depends on master data, when I import Leaves or Attendance, then the sequencing rule requires the referenced Workforce/Organizational masters to exist first; Given a transactional record references a missing employee or master, when validated, then it is reported as a record-level failure rather than silently ingested.

title: Support multiple file formats
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to import and export data in CSV, XLS, XLSX, and JSON formats, so that I can work with the file type that best fits my source systems and tools.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am importing or exporting data, when I select a format, then CSV, XLS, XLSX, and JSON are all available options.; Given a file in any of the four supported formats, when I submit it for import, then the file is accepted and parsed according to its format.; Given an export request, when I choose a format, then the output file is produced in that exact format (CSV, XLS, XLSX, or JSON).; Given a file in an unsupported format, when I submit it, then the system rejects the file with a clear message stating the supported formats.

title: Enforce file size and batch limits
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the system to enforce a 50 MB maximum file size and 10,000 records per batch, so that imports stay within safe processing limits and do not overload the platform.
acceptance_criteria: Given an import file larger than 50 MB, when I submit it, then the system rejects it and informs me of the 50 MB maximum file size limit.; Given an import file containing more than 10,000 records, when I submit it, then the system rejects the batch and informs me of the 10,000-records-per-batch limit.; Given a file within both the 50 MB size and 10,000-record limits, when I submit it, then it is accepted for processing.; Given a dataset exceeding 10,000 records, when I need to load it, then I must split it into multiple batches each within the limit.

title: Sequence imports by dependency
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want imports to follow the correct dependency sequence — Foundation Masters, then Organizational Masters, then Workforce Masters, then Transactional Data — so that dependent records are only loaded after their prerequisites exist.
acceptance_criteria: Given multiple data types to import, when I run the imports, then the system processes them in the order Foundation Masters → Organizational Masters → Workforce Masters → Transactional Data.; Given a transactional record that references a master not yet imported, when the sequence is enforced, then the prerequisite master is imported before the dependent transactional data.; Given an attempt to import data out of dependency order, when the sequence is enforced, then dependent records are not processed before their prerequisites and are reported accordingly.; Given the four defined tiers, when I review the import plan, then each entity is correctly classified into its tier (Foundation, Organizational, Workforce, or Transactional).

title: Pre-import validation with staging mode
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to run pre-import validation in a sandbox/staging mode, so that I can verify my data is correct before it is committed to production.
acceptance_criteria: Given an import file, when I choose staging/sandbox mode, then the file is validated without committing any changes to production data.; Given pre-import validation runs, when it completes, then I can review the full outcome (per-record success/failure) before deciding to proceed with the actual import.; Given validation errors are found in staging, when I review them, then I can correct the file and re-run validation before committing.; Given a file passes staging validation, when I promote it, then only the validated records are committed to production.; Given I skip staging, when I run a direct import, then pre-import validation is still applied before any commit.
source: FR 6.24.4 (Validation and Error Reporting — pre-import validation / staging mode)

title: Record-level error reporting
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want detailed error reporting with record-level success and failure results, so that I can pinpoint and fix exactly which records failed.
acceptance_criteria: Given an import has been validated or processed, when I view the results, then each record is marked individually as success or failure.; Given a failed record, when I inspect it, then the report shows the specific reason for the failure (e.g., validation rule, missing dependency, format error).; Given a completed report, when I review it, then I can clearly distinguish which records succeeded from those that failed and see counts of each.; Given a record-level error report, when I need to act on it, then I can export or download the report to correct the source data.
source: FR 6.24.4 (Validation and Error Reporting — record-level reporting)

title: Transactional rollback for failed imports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want failed imports to roll back transactionally with atomic transactions where feasible, so that a partial or failed import does not leave data in an inconsistent state.
acceptance_criteria: Given an import fails, when rollback is applied, then all changes from the failed import are reverted.; Given an import processed as an atomic transaction, when any part fails, then no records from that transaction are committed.; Given a rollback has completed, when I review the data, then it reflects the exact state prior to the failed import.; Given atomic transactions are not feasible for a given import, when a failure occurs, then the system still rolls back to a consistent state and reports which records were committed.

title: Real-time import status tracking
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want real-time status tracking for my imports, so that I always know whether a job is submitted, validating, running, completed, or failed.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have submitted an import, when I view its status, then it shows one of Submitted, Validating, In-progress, Completed, Failed, or Partially completed.; Given an import is being processed, when its state changes, then the displayed status updates in real time without a manual refresh.; Given an import finishes with some records succeeding and others failing, when I view the status, then it shows Partially completed with the success/failure breakdown.; Given an import fails outright, when I view the status, then it shows Failed with a link to the error report.; Given each defined state, when tracked, then all six statuses (Submitted, Validating, In-progress, Completed, Failed, Partially completed) are represented.

title: Bulk import and export across group companies
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to import and export master and transactional data for the companies within my group, so that I can migrate and maintain data consistently across the group's entities in one place.
acceptance_criteria: Given I administer a group of companies, when I open Data Management, then I can select which company (or companies) within my group the import/export applies to.; Given a bulk export request for the group, when it completes, then the output includes data scoped to the companies I administer within the selected format.; Given an import for a specific company in my group, when I submit it, then records are validated, sequenced, committed, and rolled back per the same rules and scoped only to that company.; Given I lack rights to a company outside my group, when I attempt import/export, then the system prevents access to that company's data.
source: FR 6.24.1; FR 6.24.3; FR 6.24.4 (Import/Export Scope — group-level administration)

title: Bulk import and export across the portfolio
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want to import and export master and transactional data across the companies in my portfolio, so that I can perform large-scale migration and bulk maintenance spanning multiple companies.
acceptance_criteria: Given I administer a portfolio of companies, when I open Data Management, then I can scope import/export to one, several, or all companies in my portfolio.; Given a portfolio-wide import, when it runs, then dependency sequencing, validation, staging, error reporting, and rollback are applied per company batch.; Given a portfolio-wide export, when it completes, then a downloadable file is produced in the selected format containing data for the chosen portfolio scope.; Given file size and batch limits, when I run large portfolio operations, then the 50 MB / 10,000-record limits are enforced per batch.
source: FR 6.24.1; FR 6.24.2; FR 6.24.5 (Import/Export Scope — portfolio-level administration)

title: Oversee platform-wide data migration
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to configure and oversee data import/export and migration across the entire platform, so that onboarding, bulk maintenance, and operational data exchange run reliably for all tenants.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am a Platform Admin, when I access Data Management, then I can perform or oversee import/export for any company on the platform.; Given platform-wide import settings, when I configure them, then supported formats (CSV, XLS, XLSX, JSON) and the 50 MB / 10,000-record limits are applied consistently across tenants.; Given migration onboarding for a new tenant, when I run imports, then the Foundation → Organizational → Workforce → Transactional sequence is enforced.; Given any import job on the platform, when I monitor it, then I can view its real-time status (Submitted, Validating, In-progress, Completed, Failed, Partially completed) and its record-level error report.; Given a failed import for any tenant, when rollback runs, then the affected tenant's data is restored to a consistent pre-import state.

title: Download error report and re-import corrected records
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to download the detailed error report and re-import only the corrected records after a partial or failed import, so that I can efficiently complete an import without re-processing already-succeeded records.
acceptance_criteria: Given an import completes with failures or is Partially completed, when I open the results, then I can download the record-level error report in a supported format.; Given a downloaded error report, when I correct the flagged records, then I can re-submit a corrected file for import.; Given a re-import of corrected records, when it runs, then previously succeeded records are not duplicated and validation/sequencing/rollback rules still apply.; Given the correction cycle, when I re-run in staging first, then I can confirm all errors are resolved before committing.
source: FR 6.24.4; FR 6.24.6 (Error Reporting / Partially completed handling)

title: Enforce uniqueness and referential integrity on import
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want imported records to be matched, deduplicated, and integrity-checked against the canonical data model, so that bulk loads never create duplicate masters or orphaned references.
acceptance_criteria: Given an import record whose natural/business key already exists, when it is persisted, then the existing record is updated (upsert) rather than a duplicate being created.; Given a transactional or child record that references a parent master, when it is persisted, then referential integrity is enforced and records with a non-existent parent reference are rejected as record-level failures.; Given uniqueness constraints on master entities (e.g., a company/department/location key), when two import rows collide on the same key, then the conflict is reported and the constraint is not violated in storage.; Given all imported records, when they are committed, then they are written within the correct tenant scope so no record is attributed to the wrong company.

title: Preserve effective-dating and history for imported records
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want historical transactional and master records to be stored with their correct effective dates in the bitemporal data model, so that migrated history is queryable as-of any past date and later corrections do not overwrite the past.
acceptance_criteria: Given historical Leave/Attendance or master records carrying as-of/effective dates, when they are imported, then each is persisted with its stated effective date rather than the load date.; Given the bitemporal model, when a later import corrects a previously loaded record, then a new version is created and prior history is retained rather than being overwritten.; Given imported history, when I query the data as-of a past date, then the record values reflect what was effective on that date.; Given overlapping effective-dated rows for the same entity, when validated, then conflicting or gapped date ranges are flagged as record-level errors.

title: Tenant-scoped isolation of import and export
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want every import and export to be strictly tenant-scoped by row-level security, so that no bulk operation can read or write another tenant's data.
acceptance_criteria: Given an import job for a specific tenant/company, when records are written, then RLS confines all writes to that tenant's scope and cross-tenant writes are impossible.; Given an export request, when it runs, then only records within the caller's authorized tenant/company scope are returned, regardless of the file the caller crafted.; Given a portfolio- or group-scoped operation, when it executes, then each record is isolated to its owning company within that scope and never bleeds across sibling tenants.; Given an attempt to reference an out-of-scope company id inside an import file, when it is processed, then those rows are rejected rather than silently redirected.
source: FR 6.24.1 (Purpose — multi-tenant); FR 6.24.5 (L1 domain — tenant-scoped storage / RLS)

title: Govern import/export config as versioned metadata
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the supported formats, size/batch limits, and entity-to-tier classification to be governed as versioned, effective-dated configuration, so that the import engine's behavior can change without code and stay consistent across tenants.
acceptance_criteria: Given import configuration, when I define the supported formats (CSV, XLS, XLSX, JSON), the 50 MB / 10,000-record limits, and the tier (Foundation/Organizational/Workforce/Transactional) classification of each entity, then the import/export engine reads this governed config at runtime.; Given a config change, when it is published, then it is versioned and effective-dated so prior job runs remain explainable and the change takes effect without a code release.; Given per-tenant overrides are permitted, when a tenant needs a different limit or format policy, then the override is applied only within that tenant's scope while defaults govern the rest.; Given the dependency-sequencing tiers, when the entity classification config changes, then the sequencing engine orders imports according to the new configuration.
source: FR 6.24.2; FR 6.24.3 (L2 config — governed formats/limits/tier classification)

title: Guided import wizard and live job monitoring dashboard
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a metadata-driven import/export wizard and a live job-monitoring dashboard, so that I can self-serve bulk data operations and track them without technical help.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Data Management screen, when I start an import, then a guided wizard walks me through entity selection, format and file upload, staging preview, and final commit.; Given a running or completed job, when I open the monitoring dashboard, then a grid lists all my import/export jobs with live status (Submitted, Validating, In-progress, Completed, Failed, Partially completed) that updates without manual refresh.; Given a job with results, when I view its row, then I can inline-download the output file and, for failures, the record-level error report.; Given the staging preview, when validation errors are shown, then the UI highlights the failing records and reasons before I choose to commit.
source: FR 6.24.4; FR 6.24.6 (L4 presentation — self-service import UX / job dashboard)
